factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is present, particularly in the Aerospace and defense segments, with the Information Technology segment also showing growth due to the CSRA acquisition.

evidence.text: Our consolidated revenue increased in the second quarter and first six months of 2019 driven by additional aircraft deliveries in our Aerospace segment and organic growth in our defense segments. In the first six months of 2019, the increase in revenue was also driven by the acquisition of CSRA Inc. (CSRA) in our Information Technology segment, which we acquired on April 3, 2018.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic growth is present in key segments, and an acquisition contributed to revenue increases.

factors.summary: Input costs are increasing, particularly in the Aerospace segment, which is impacting operating margins.

evidence.text: Product operating costs increased at a higher rate than revenue due primarily to the shift in mix of Gulfstream aircraft deliveries.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable and not creating significant margin pressure.

evidence.text: As a percentage of revenue, G&A expenses were 6.4% in the first six months of 2019 compared with 6.9% in the first six months of 2018. We expect G&A expenses as a percentage of revenue in 2019 to be generally consistent with 2018.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are manageable and not causing significant margin pressure.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to shifts in product mix and increased costs in certain segments.

evidence.text: Operating margin decreased 80 basis points in the second quarter and 190 basis points in the first six months of 2019 driven by contract mix in our U.S. military vehicles business and an unfavorable settlement in the first quarter of 2019 relating to a lease at a former operating site outside the United States.

sentiment.label: negative

factors.summary: Management has maintained forward guidance for revenue and margins, with slight adjustments expected for 2019.

evidence.text: We expect 2019 revenue in the Aerospace segment to be $9.95 billion and operating earnings to be $1.525 billion. We expect the Information Technology segment’s 2019 revenue to be approximately $8.5 billion, a slight increase from 2018, reflecting a full year of CSRA’s results, offset partially by divestiture activities. We expect the segment’s operating margin to be around 7.4%. We expect the Marine Systems segment’s 2019 revenue to be approximately $9 billion, and operating margin is expected to be around 8.5%.

factors.summary: The backlog orders show a mixed trend, with a slight decrease in total backlog and defense segments, but stability in the aerospace segment and overall estimated contract value.

evidence.text: The Aerospace segment ended the second quarter of 2019 with backlog of $12.15 billion compared with $12.17 billion on March 31, 2019. Total backlog in our defense segments was $55.5 billion on June 30, 2019, compared with $57 billion on March 31, 2019.

Our total backlog, including funded and unfunded portions, was $67.7 billion at the end of the second quarter of 2019, down 2.2% from $69.2 billion on March 31, 2019. Our total estimated contract value was $101.9 billion on June 30, 2019, compared with $103.2 billion on March 31, 2019.

factors.summary: The contract backlog is generally stable, with slight decreases observed in both aerospace and defense segments, though new orders are outpacing revenue in some areas.

evidence.text: Aerospace funded backlog represents new aircraft and custom completion orders for which we have definitive purchase contracts and deposits from customers. Unfunded backlog consists of agreements to provide future aircraft maintenance and support services. The Aerospace segment ended the second quarter of 2019 with backlog of $12.15 billion compared with $12.17 billion on March 31, 2019. Total backlog in our defense segments was $55.5 billion on June 30, 2019, compared with $57 billion on March 31, 2019.
Our total backlog, including funded and unfunded portions, was $67.7 billion at the end of the second quarter of 2019, down 2.2% from $69.2 billion on March 31, 2019.
